name: Administrator rights available
description: Detect whether script is running with administrative privileges
rationale: Some checks require admin rights to be accurate.
notes: If not admin, present a warning and run limited checks.

name: System version detected
description: Capture OS version and build for profile selection
rationale: Profile selection requires accurate OS version.
notes: Store version string for future profile mapping.

name: User and PATH info collected
description: Collect primary user, PATH and common environment variables
rationale: Context for further checks and detection confidence.
notes: Non-sensitive subset only; avoid exfiltration of secrets.

name: IPv4 addresses collected
description: Capture IPv4 interface addresses
rationale: Network context for risk and scope decisions.
notes: Use fallback to ipconfig on older hosts.

name: IPv6 addresses collected
description: Capture IPv6 interface addresses
rationale: IPv6 may expose additional attack paths.
notes: Fallback to ipconfig on older hosts.

name: Windows Update configured
description: Check Windows AutoUpdate configuration and service state
rationale: Windows Update presence is required for patching; disabled updates increase exposure.
notes: Systems managed by WSUS may have manual/service states; check policy keys.

name: Check for missing critical/important updates
description: Detect applicable missing critical/important updates
audit_method: Use Get-WindowsUpdateLog or Windows Update API (WUA) query; fallback: use presence of recent security KBs
rationale: Unpatched critical updates are high-risk.
notes: Implementation may require WSUS/WUA bindings; mark as advisory if offline.

name: BitLocker status
description: Check BitLocker encryption status for system and data volumes
rationale: Disk encryption reduces data-at-rest compromise impact.
notes: May be not applicable to all devices; treat as improvement if absent.

name: AlwaysInstallElevated disabled (registry)
description: Ensure 'AlwaysInstallElevated' registry keys are not set (MSI elevation risk)
audit_method: reg query "HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer" /v AlwaysInstallElevated & reg query "HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer" /v AlwaysInstallElevated
rationale: If set, non-admins can elevate via MSI packages.
notes: Missing key = default (0).

name: PowerShell logging: module logging enabled
description: Ensure PowerShell Module Logging is enabled
rationale: Module logging increases forensic visibility of PowerShell activity.
notes: Also verify specific modules list if required.

name: PowerShell logging: script block logging enabled
description: Ensure script block logging is enabled
rationale: Script block logging captures obfuscated PowerShell code.
notes: Heavy logging may increase storage costs.

name: PowerShell logging: transcription/invoke header enabled
description: Ensure PowerShell transcription or invocation header logging is enabled
rationale: Transcription aids incident response.
notes: Check transcription path exists and access controls are in place.

name: PowerShell protected event logging
description: Ensure PowerShell protected event logging is enabled (where available)
rationale: Protects logs from tampering on modern OSes.
notes: Only present on newer PowerShell/OS versions.

name: PowerShell event log sizes configured
description: Check PowerShell/Windows PowerShell event log configured sizes/retention
rationale: Ensure audit logs retain useful history.
notes: Adjust size based on storage policy.

name: Critical Windows Event logs max sizes
description: Ensure Application/System/Security logs have max sizes set
audit_method: wevtutil gl Application; wevtutil gl System; wevtutil gl Security | parse MaximumSizeInBytes
rationale: Prevent logs from being trivially overwritten.
notes: Values are examples; set per policy.

name: Event logs: specific operational logs present
description: Verify targeted operational logs are enabled (TaskScheduler, SMBServer, WinRM, WMI-Activity, Netlogon, TerminalServices)
audit_method: Check existence and enabled state of 'Microsoft-Windows-TaskScheduler/Operational', 'Microsoft-Windows-SMBServer/Audit', 'Microsoft-Windows-WinRM/Operational', 'Microsoft-Windows-WMI-Activity/Operational', 'Microsoft-Windows-Security-Netlogon/Operational', 'Microsoft-Windows-TerminalServices-LocalSessionManager/Operational'
rationale: These logs are crucial for detecting attacks and lateral movement.
notes: Some logs may not exist on older SKUs; mark as NA when not present.

name: PowerShell version and v2 disabled
description: Check PowerShell default version and that legacy v2 is not allowed
rationale: PowerShell v2 lacks modern security features; disable where possible.
notes: Windows 7 may require alternative checks for installed .NET versions.

name: Cached credentials count
description: Check number of cached domain credentials permitted
rationale: Reducing cached creds reduces credential replay risk.
notes: 0 may be impractical in some AD environments; document exceptions.

name: RDP remote connections configuration
description: Check RDP enablement and fDenyTSConnections
audit_method: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server" /v fDenyTSConnections; Get-Service -Name TermService
rationale: Disable RDP or restrict it to reduce remote breach surface.
notes: Servers or admin hosts may be exceptions; use role_scope to filter.

name: AllowRemoteRPC / Remote Registry config
description: Check AllowRemoteRPC and RemoteRegistry service
rationale: Remote registry exposes sensitive configuration remotely.
notes: If remote management required, ensure secure channels and ACLs.

name: WinRM configuration and service
description: Check WinRM listener and service, and firewall rules for management
audit_method: Get-Service -Name WinRM; winrm enumerate winrm/config/Listener; Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName '*WinRM*' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
rationale: WinRM should be disabled or strongly restricted on endpoints.
notes: Management servers will be exempt.

name: Local Administrator account enumeration
description: Detect number of accounts in Local Administrators group
audit_method: Get-LocalGroupMember -Group Administrators OR net localgroup Administrators
rationale: Excess local admins increase lateral attack capability.
notes: Domain-joined management accounts may be present; review exceptions.

name: AppLocker / script control
description: Check AppLocker or script enforcement (where supported)
audit_method: Get-AppLockerPolicy -Effective -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Get-CimInstance -Namespace root\Microsoft\Windows\AppLocker -ClassName MSFT_AppLockerPolicy -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
rationale: Application control reduces script-based attacks.
notes: AppLocker requires Enterprise/Server SKUs on some OSes.

name: EMET / Exploit mitigation presence (legacy)
description: Detect EMET or OS exploit mitigations presence for older systems
audit_method: Check installed programs for EMET or presence of EMET service; on Win10 verify OS mitigations
rationale: EMET used on older hosts; presence optional but indicates mitigation.
notes: EMET is deprecated; for Win10 rely on built-in mitigations.

name: LAPS installed
description: Detect if Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) is installed
audit_method: Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name LAPS; Check for scheduled task or GPO setting
rationale: LAPS reduces risk from reused local admin passwords.
notes: If not installed, recommend as improvement; not mandatory.

name: Group Policy refresh enforcement
description: Check NoGPOListChanges and related settings to ensure policies reapply
rationale: Ensure GPO changes reapply correctly.
notes: Default = 0; domain-managed machines may differ.

name: Disable Net Session Enumeration (NetSessEnum)
description: Verify Net Session Enumeration permissions are locked to admins
audit_method: Run provided NetSessEnumPerm.ps1 / query session enumeration ACLs
rationale: Restricting session enumeration reduces info leakage.
notes: Script required to evaluate ACLs accurately.

name: Disable WPAD
description: Ensure WPAD (Web Proxy Auto-Discovery) is not set in hosts or registry
audit_method: Check hosts file for 'wpad' entries; reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\WinHTTP\Parameters" /v AutoConfigURL
rationale: WPAD can be abused for proxy-based credential capture.
notes: Network proxies managed centrally may be legitimate; validate exceptions.

name: WinHTTP AutoProxy service
description: Check WinHTTPAutoProxySvc is not running unless required
rationale: Auto-proxy services increase attack surface for proxy insertion.
notes: Some enterprise proxy setups may require it.

name: KB3165191 installed (Mitigation check placeholder)
description: Check for presence of KB3165191 or other specific mitigation KBs (example)
rationale: Example check for specific mitigation KB.
notes: Replace with actual KBs relevant to your environment.

name: WINS configuration
description: Detect legacy WINS or LMHOSTS usage and report
audit_method: Get-NetAdapter | Get-NetIPInterface -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; netsh interface ip show config
rationale: WINS is legacy and increases attack surface.
notes: Legacy networks may require WINS; document exceptions.

name: DNS: Disable LLMNR
description: Ensure LLMNR is disabled to reduce name poisoning attacks
rationale: LLMNR facilitates credential theft via name poisoning.
notes: Group Policy recommended for rollout.

name: Computer Browser service disabled
description: Ensure Computer Browser (Browser) service is stopped/disabled
rationale: Browser service is legacy and rarely required.
notes: OK to disable in modern networks.

name: NetBIOS over TCP/IP disabled
description: Verify NetBIOS (TcpipNetbiosOptions) is disabled
audit_method: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces\<ifIndex>" /v TcpipNetbiosOptions OR use Get-NetAdapterBinding
rationale: Disabling NetBIOS reduces legacy attack surface.
notes: Interface-level setting; evaluate per-adapter.

name: Windows Scripting Host disabled (WSH)
description: Check if Windows Script Host is disabled via registry
rationale: Disabling WSH reduces script-based malware risk where not required.
notes: Breaks legacy administration scripts; use with caution.

name: Prevent interactive token filter (LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy)
description: Ensure LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy is set to restrict interactive remote admin token usage
rationale: Restrict remote local account privileges to reduce lateral attacks.
notes: Domain-managed environments may require exceptions.

name: Disable WDigest credential caching
description: Ensure WDigest.UseLogonCredential set to 0
rationale: WDigest caching exposes plaintext credentials in memory.
notes: Apply KBs that backport mitigation where required.

name: Disable SMBv1
description: Ensure SMBv1 server and client are disabled
rationale: SMBv1 is insecure and exploited in major incidents.
notes: Some legacy appliances may require temporary exceptions.

name: Block untrusted fonts (Windows 10)
description: Check block untrusted fonts mitigation (Kernel MitigationOptions)
rationale: Prevents font-based code execution on modern OS.
notes: Only applicable to Windows 10+.

name: Credential Guard / Device Guard presence (Windows 10)
description: Detect Credential Guard or Device Guard configured and running
rationale: Credential Guard reduces credential theft risk on supported hardware.
notes: Requires hardware/vTPM and enterprise configurations.

name: Lsa NoLmHash
description: Ensure Lsa.NoLmHash registry key is enabled (do not store LM hashes)
rationale: Prevents storage of legacy LM password hashes.
notes: Older environments may have compatibility issues.

name: Lsa LMCompatibilityLevel
description: Ensure Lsa.LmCompatibilityLevel is configured to require NTLMv2
rationale: Enforce NTLMv2 and refuse LM/NTLM where possible.
notes: Domain-level policy preferred.

name: Restrict anonymous enumeration
description: Ensure anonymous enumeration of domain/local info is restricted
rationale: Reduce info leakage to unauthenticated users.
notes: Some legacy applications may expect looser settings.

name: NTLM session security
description: Ensure NTLM session security requires NTLMv2 and 128-bit encryption
audit_method: secedit /export /cfg c:\temp\secpol.cfg; parse [System Access] or check GPOs for 'Network security: Configure encryption types allowed for Kerberos' and NTLM settings
rationale: Stronger NTLM settings reduce credential relay/weakness attacks.
notes: Domain GPOs are authoritative.

name: SMBv1 auditing if present
description: If SMBv1 is present, ensure auditing is enabled for SMB server
rationale: If SMBv1 cannot be removed, increase auditing to detect misuse.
notes: Prefer removal over audit.

name: Windows Firewall (MpsSvc) running and BFE present
description: Ensure Windows Firewall and Base Filtering Engine are present and running
rationale: Firewall and BFE are core network defense primitives.
notes: If third-party firewall is in use, verify vendor service and telemetry.

name: Windows Defender / AV presence
description: Verify Defender or enterprise AV/EDR agent is installed and reporting
audit_method: Get-Service -Name WinDefend -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Check registry/service for known EDR agents
rationale: Active endpoint protection reduces detection/response times.
notes: Multiple engines can conflict; verify vendor guidance.

name: TrustedInstaller (Windows Modules Installer) present
description: Ensure TrustedInstaller service exists (for component servicing) and is not disabled
rationale: TrustedInstaller is required for proper patching; ensure not maliciously removed.
notes: Status variations acceptable; presence is key.

name: Windows Update related services (BITS) present
description: Check BITS and related services needed for updates
rationale: BITS used by Windows Update; required for patch delivery.
notes: Service may be paused; check update backend if WSUS is used.

name: Windows Installer hotfix presence (e.g., KB2871997)
description: Check for presence of legacy hotfixes that address privilege escalation or service issues
rationale: Example KB check — replace with relevant KBs per environment.
notes: Hotfix checks are advisory; prefer broader missing updates check.
